winner: Why did the shirt roll downhill? Because it heard there was a big sale at the hill-arious roller-coaster store!

candidates.text: Why did the shirt roll up a hill? It heard there was no more downhill thread.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Creative wordplay with 'thread' and 'downhill,' good use of required words, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the shirt roll downhill? Because it heard there was a big sale at the hill-arious roller-coaster store!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "Includes required words, uses a pun on 'hill-arious,' but the joke is somewhat forced and the connection between the shirt rolling and the sale is weak."}

candidates.text: Why don't rollercoasters ever argue with each other? Because they always know how to button up their shirts!
When I saw my dog rolling around in a pile of dirt, I thought, "Oh no, he's getting dirty again." But then I realized, at least his shirt was already dirty.
Why don't roller coasters ever argue with each other? Because they always find common ground - on your shirt after you ride!
